skill.name: style-audit
skill.description: Audits code against CI/CD style rules, quality guidelines, and best practices, then rewrites code to meet standards without breaking functionality. Use this skill after functionality validation to ensure code is not just correct but also maintainable, readable, and production-ready. The skill applies linting rules, enforces naming conventions, improves code organization, and refactors for clarity while preserving all behavioral correctness verified by functionality audits.
skill.summary: Improve working code with linting, refactoring, documentation, and style review while preserving behavior.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but review found documentation text and diagram labels only. No executable code, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md or style-audit-process.dot.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits are prose about code quality, documentation, naming, refactoring, and CI practices. They do not define or invoke weak cryptographic algorithms, and the duplicate report at SKILL.md line 60 is also documentation guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are natural-language audit guidance or DOT labels, not code. No hash function, cipher, crypto API, or downgrade instruction appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance hits describe ordinary code review topics such as performance, error handling, naming, nesting, and team preferences. They do not instruct the agent to enumerate secrets, collect host data, or exfiltrate environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is code quality review, not host or network reconnaissance. The skill contains no commands for inventorying systems, reading sensitive files, or sending collected data elsewhere.

content.value_statement: Working code can still be hard to maintain, review, or deploy. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through linting, refactoring, documentation review, and style cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Reviews code against linting, formatting, naming, and organization standards.
Prioritizes style issues by security, readability, maintainability, and performance impact.
Guides safe refactoring while preserving behavior with tests and incremental verification.
Improves error handling, documentation, comments, and type clarity.
Produces structured reports with findings, refactoring summaries, remaining issues, and metrics.
limitations: It depends on available project tests and linting tools for automated verification.
It cannot know private team preferences unless those standards are provided.
Large repositories may require phased review instead of one complete pass.
It is not a replacement for human review of architecture or product intent.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Clean up formatting, naming, decomposition, and documentation before reviewers evaluate the change.

use_cases.title: Standardize Inherited Code
use_cases.description: Audit working legacy code and plan focused improvements that make future changes easier.

use_cases.title: Enforce CI Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Align code with linting, type checking, complexity, and documentation expectations before CI runs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Style Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after code works and before a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these files for style, readability, and maintainability. Report the main issues and suggest safe improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Safe Fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the available style checks, apply low-risk formatting and lint fixes, then rerun tests and summarize changes.

prompt_templates.title: Team Standards Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use with a project style guide or CI rules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this code against our style guide and CI rules. Prioritize issues that block review or deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Refactoring Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for complex code that needs clearer structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this working code for clarity and maintainability. Preserve behavior, document each significant change, and list remaining risks.

output_examples.input: A working feature branch with inconsistent formatting and unclear helper names.
output_examples.output: The audit identifies formatting drift, vague names, and oversized functions. It recommends automated formatting first, then targeted renaming and extraction.

output_examples.input: A legacy module with weak documentation and hard-to-follow error handling.
output_examples.output: The report groups issues by documentation, error handling, and complexity. It explains which changes are safe now and which need owner review.

output_examples.input: A pull request that must pass strict CI style checks.
output_examples.output: The skill runs style checks, fixes mechanical issues, reruns tests, and summarizes remaining warnings for the reviewer.

best_practices: Run functionality tests before and after style changes.
Provide the project style guide, linter configuration, and CI expectations.
Review generated refactors in small batches with clear commit messages.
anti_patterns: Do not request broad rewrites without tests or acceptance criteria.
Do not mix style cleanup with unrelated feature changes.
Do not override team conventions with generic style preferences.

faq.question: Does this skill write code changes?
faq.answer: Yes, it can guide safe rewrites when the agent has editing permission and tests are available.

faq.question: Can it run linters automatically?
faq.answer: It recommends and uses project linting tools when they are installed and appropriate for the language.

faq.question: Does it replace a functionality audit?
faq.answer: No. It is intended to run after functionality has been validated.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Provide target files, style rules, linter configuration, test commands, and review priorities.

faq.question: Can it audit security style issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes secure coding review as part of maintainability and production readiness.

faq.question: Is it suitable for large repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but large repositories usually need scoped audits or phased cleanup plans.
